codeReview.summary: The new park-factor and ABS helper modules correctly load configured values, normalize exception venue names, and centralize framing retention, but the end-to-end game-row path still does not satisfy VAL-FEAT-006. When odds backfill has to synthesize a missing game, it derives the venue from the home team's default stadium instead of the actual neutral-site venue, so Mexico City / Field of Dreams / Little League Classic games can still be persisted as ABS-active.

issues.description: `_ensure_game_row()` builds synthetic `games` rows from only `home_team_name`/`away_team_name`, sets `venue` from `STADIUMS_BY_TEAM_CODE[home_team_code]['park_name']` at lines 229-235, and then persists that derived venue plus `is_abs_active` at lines 249-268. For neutral-site exception games, that means a backfilled row will use the ordinary home ballpark (for example, Yankee Stadium) rather than the actual Mexico City / Field of Dreams / Little League Classic venue, so `is_abs_active` stays true even though VAL-FEAT-006 requires exception-venue games to store `abs_active = False` in the database. The handoff/test coverage only proves the pure helper returns `False` for a Mexico City string, not that backfilled game rows honor the exception.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared Windows setup guidance is still inconsistent. This worker again had to deviate from the default init/install flow because `environment.md` documents using the project interpreter directly on Windows, while the shared init script and primary install command still rely on bare `pip` after shell activation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback in C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T18-09-29-837Z__t13-park-abs-adjustments__e61ea406-cd30-4c6d-9e0c-25ee870b588b.json records the justified init deviation; compare C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\environment.md:59-60, C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\services.yaml:5-6, and C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\init.sh:24.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state documents that ABS exception venues exist, but it does not note that the current odds backfill path synthesizes `venue` from the home team's configured park when a game row is missing. Future workers could therefore assume exception handling is already database-safe when it is only helper-safe unless another data source has populated the real venue first.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\architecture.md:79 notes the exception venues at a high level, while C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\src\clients\odds_client.py:224-235 and 249-268 show the backfill path deriving venue from `STADIUMS_BY_TEAM_CODE` and persisting that synthetic value.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the t13 handoff, transcript skeleton, feature-worker skill, shared-state docs, and commit e37b3739b2d75b99e2bc614857e562671517c559. The helper modules themselves look good, but the odds backfill integration still stores the home team's normal stadium for synthetic games, so ABS exception venues are not reliably persisted as `is_abs_active = False` and the feature does not fully satisfy VAL-FEAT-006.
